ddls:
CREATE TABLE "legal_subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each legal subject */,
  "code" TEXT /* Unique code for the legal subject */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the legal subject */,
  "parent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent legal subject */,
  "definition" TEXT /* Definition of the legal subject */,
  "notes_on_definition" TEXT /* Notes on the definition of the legal subject */,
  "synonyms" TEXT /* Synonyms for the legal subject */,
  "examples" TEXT /* Examples of the legal subject */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the legal subject (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the legal subject */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the legal subject was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the legal subject */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the legal subject was last updated */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Flag to mark if the legal subject is deleted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_legal_subjects_parent_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "legal_subject_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each legal subject type */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the legal subject */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the legal subject type */,
  "definition" TEXT /* Definition of the legal subject type */,
  "notes_on_definition" TEXT /* Notes on the definition of the legal subject type */,
  "synonyms" TEXT /* Synonyms for the legal subject type */,
  "examples" TEXT /* Examples of the legal subject type */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the legal subject type (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the legal subject type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the legal subject type was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the legal subject type */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the legal subject type was last updated */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Flag to mark if the legal subject type is deleted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_legal_subject_types_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., lawyer, paralegal, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to mark if the user is active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subject_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the legal subject being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the legal subject */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the legal subject was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subject_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subject_access_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subject_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "parent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent legal subject */,
  "child_id" INTEGER /* ID of the child legal subject */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., parent-child, sibling) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the relationship was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the relationship */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subject_relationships_child_id FOREIGN KEY ("child_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subject_relationships_parent_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "definitions" (
  "definition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each definition */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the legal subject */,
  "definition_text" TEXT /* Definition text for the legal subject */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the definition was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the definition was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the definition */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the definition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("definition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_definitions_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "definition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the definition */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Note text on the definition */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the note */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the note */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notes_definition_id FOREIGN KEY ("definition_id") REFERENCES definitions ("definition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "synonyms" (
  "synonym_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each synonym */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the legal subject */,
  "synonym_text" TEXT /* Synonym text for the legal subject */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the synonym was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the synonym was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the synonym */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the synonym */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("synonym_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_synonyms_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "examples" (
  "example_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each example */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the legal subject */,
  "example_text" TEXT /* Example text for the legal subject */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the example was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the example was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the example */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the example */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("example_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examples_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subject_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the legal subject */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the legal subject */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Flag to mark if the version is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subject_versions_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES legal_subjects ("subject_id")
)
